annotations.label: Differential form

anchor.snippet: differential forms provide a unified approach to define integrands over curves

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated 'differential form' type; the library represents an n-form as a function `E → E [⋀^Fin n]→L[𝕜] F` using `ContinuousAlternatingMap`.

annotations.label: Volume form

anchor.snippet: is called a volume form

annotations.note: `Orientation.volumeForm` is the nonvanishing top-degree alternating form on an oriented inner-product space.

annotations.label: Differential forms form an alternating algebra

anchor.snippet: The differential forms form an alternating algebra

annotations.note: The algebraic exterior algebra and its graded structure are formalized, but no graded-algebra structure on the space of differential forms themselves is.

annotations.label: Exterior derivative (informal)

anchor.snippet: The exterior derivative is an operation on differential forms

annotations.note: `extDeriv` defines the exterior derivative of a differential n-form on a normed space.

annotations.label: Generalized Stokes theorem (informal)

anchor.snippet: the generalized Stokes theorem

annotations.note: Only the divergence theorem on rectangles is formalized; no general Stokes theorem for differential forms on manifolds exists.

annotations.label: Integration of a k-form over an oriented k-manifold

anchor.snippet: A differential k -form can be integrated over an oriented manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of the integral of a differential k-form over an oriented k-manifold.

annotations.label: Sign reversal under orientation reversal

anchor.snippet: which is the negative of the integral of the same differential form over the same interval

annotations.note: Only the 1D version (swap of integration limits) is formalized; no general orientation-reversal lemma for k-form integrals exists.

annotations.label: Basis of differential 1-forms

anchor.snippet: can be used as a basis for all 1 -forms

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of `dx^i` as a basis of 1-forms on a coordinate chart.

annotations.label: Basis of differential 2-forms

anchor.snippet: can be used as a basis at every point on the manifold for all 2 -forms

annotations.note: No `dx^i ∧ dx^j` basis of 2-forms is defined.

annotations.label: Exterior product (wedge product)

anchor.snippet: This is similar to the cross product from vector calculus, in that it is an alternating product

annotations.note: The wedge product of (algebraic) alternating maps is `AlternatingMap.domCoprod`, but no wedge of differential forms is defined.

annotations.label: Alternating property of the wedge

anchor.snippet: Alternating also implies that

annotations.note: Alternating maps satisfy `map_swap` (negation under swap of two inputs); a corresponding skew-symmetry of the wedge of differential forms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Multi-index notation for wedge products

anchor.snippet: A common notation for the wedge product of elementary

annotations.note: No multi-index `dx^I` notation for wedge products is defined.

annotations.label: Dimension of the module of k-forms

anchor.snippet: By calculating the size of

annotations.note: The algebraic identification of k-fold alternating maps with linear maps from the k-th exterior power exists, but the explicit C(n,k) dimension count for differential k-forms is not stated.

annotations.label: Exterior derivative of a differential form

anchor.snippet: The exterior derivative of a differential form is a generalization of the differential of a function

annotations.note: `extDeriv` defines the exterior derivative of a differential form on a normed space.

annotations.label: Exterior derivative as grad/curl/div via Hodge star

anchor.snippet: the exterior derivative corresponds to gradient

annotations.note: No Hodge star is defined, and consequently no correspondence between `d` and grad/curl/div is formalized.

annotations.label: Differential 0-form

anchor.snippet: is defined to be a smooth function

annotations.note: 0-forms are encoded via `ContinuousAlternatingMap.constOfIsEmpty` over `Fin 0`, as used in `extDeriv_constOfIsEmpty`, but there is no top-level type for 0-forms.

annotations.label: Directional derivative

anchor.snippet: has a directional derivative

annotations.note: `lineDeriv` is the directional derivative of `f` at `x` in direction `v`.

annotations.label: Directional derivative is linear in v

anchor.snippet: is a linear function of

annotations.note: When `f` is Fréchet differentiable, `lineDeriv` equals `fderiv 𝕜 f x v`, which is linear in `v` by construction; no direct `lineDeriv`-is-linear lemma is stated as such.

annotations.label: Differential df_p of a function

anchor.snippet: is denoted df p and called the derivative or differential

annotations.note: `fderiv 𝕜 f x` is the Fréchet derivative/differential at the point `x`.

annotations.label: General differential 1-form

anchor.snippet: we define the differential 1 -form

annotations.note: A 1-form is encoded as `E → E [⋀^Fin 1]→L[𝕜] F`; no dedicated type for 1-forms is defined.

annotations.label: Necessary condition dα = 0 for exactness

anchor.snippet: is a necessary condition for the existence of a function

annotations.note: The fact `d² = 0` is `extDeriv_extDeriv`, which implies the necessary condition, but the statement that exactness implies closedness is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Exterior derivative dα of a 1-form

anchor.snippet: This 2 -form is called the exterior derivative

annotations.note: `extDeriv_apply` gives an explicit formula for the exterior derivative, specializing to 1-forms by taking `n = 1`.

annotations.label: Space of differential k-forms (in coordinates)

anchor.snippet: there is a space of differential k -forms

annotations.note: The space of k-forms is implicit as `E → E [⋀^Fin k]→L[𝕜] F`; it is not given a bundled name in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth differential form of degree k

anchor.snippet: A smooth differential form of degree

annotations.note: A smooth k-form is encoded as a `ContDiff 𝕜 ⊤ ω` function `E → E [⋀^Fin k]→L[𝕜] F`; no bundled smooth-form type is defined.

annotations.label: Differential form as alternating multilinear map

anchor.snippet: this is equivalently an alternating multilinear map

annotations.note: `AlternatingMap R M N ι` is the type of alternating multilinear maps.

annotations.label: Alternation map

anchor.snippet: The alternation map is defined as a mapping

annotations.note: `MultilinearMap.alternatization` is the antisymmetrization sending a multilinear map to its alternating part.

annotations.label: Forms as totally antisymmetric covariant tensor fields

anchor.snippet: as a totally antisymmetric covariant tensor field

annotations.note: Mathlib has no equivalence between differential forms and totally antisymmetric covariant tensor fields.

annotations.label: Exterior product of differential forms

anchor.snippet: The exterior product of a

annotations.note: Wedge product of algebraic alternating maps is formalized as `AlternatingMap.domCoprod`; no pointwise wedge product of differential forms is defined.

annotations.label: Bilinearity of the exterior product

anchor.snippet: The exterior product is bilinear

annotations.note: `domCoprod` is bilinear by construction, but no bilinearity lemma is stated for the wedge of differential forms specifically.

annotations.label: Skew (graded) commutativity of the exterior product

anchor.snippet: It is skew commutative

annotations.note: No graded commutativity lemma for the wedge of forms is stated.

annotations.label: Graded Leibniz rule

anchor.snippet: One also has the graded Leibniz rule

annotations.note: No graded Leibniz rule `d(α ∧ β) = dα ∧ β + (-1)^p α ∧ dβ` is formalized.

annotations.label: Hodge star operator and codifferential

anchor.snippet: the Hodge star operator

annotations.note: Neither the Hodge star nor the codifferential is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Clifford algebra of (co)tangent space

anchor.snippet: each (co)tangent space generates a Clifford algebra

annotations.note: `CliffordAlgebra` of an arbitrary quadratic-form module is defined, but no specialization to (co)tangent spaces of a Riemannian manifold exists.

annotations.label: Weyl algebra of vector field derivations

anchor.snippet: The (noncommutative) algebra of differential operators they generate is the Weyl algebra

annotations.note: The Weyl algebra is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: d² = 0

anchor.snippet: One important property of the exterior derivative

annotations.note: `extDeriv_extDeriv` states that the second exterior derivative of a sufficiently smooth differential form is zero.

annotations.label: de Rham complex

anchor.snippet: This complex is called the de Rham complex

annotations.note: Mathlib has no de Rham complex of a smooth manifold (the `BDeRham` file is in p-adic Hodge theory, unrelated).

annotations.label: Poincaré lemma

anchor.snippet: By the Poincaré lemma

annotations.note: The Poincaré lemma (closed forms on contractible domains are exact) is not formalized.

annotations.label: de Rham's theorem

anchor.snippet: de Rham cohomology computes the sheaf cohomology

annotations.note: De Rham's theorem comparing de Rham cohomology with sheaf cohomology is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pushforward of a tangent vector

anchor.snippet: This map is also denoted f ∗ and called the pushforward

annotations.note: `mfderiv` is the manifold (Fréchet) derivative, i.e., the pushforward of tangent vectors.

annotations.label: Pullback of a differential form

anchor.snippet: called the pullback of

annotations.note: The pullback operation appears implicitly via `compContinuousLinearMap` in `extDeriv_pullback`, but no bundled pullback of forms is defined.

annotations.label: Pullback respects basic operations on forms

anchor.snippet: Pullback respects all of the basic operations on forms

annotations.note: `extDeriv_pullback` proves the pullback commutes with `d`; compatibility with the wedge product is not formalized.

annotations.label: Integral of an n-form on Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: This allows us to define the integral of

annotations.note: No definition of the integral of an n-form on Euclidean space is given.

annotations.label: Integral of a form over a parametrized manifold

anchor.snippet: defines the integral of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no integral of a form over a parametrized submanifold.

annotations.label: k-chain

anchor.snippet: is a formal sum of smooth embeddings

annotations.note: Smooth k-chains are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Integral over a manifold via triangulation

anchor.snippet: every smooth manifold may be smoothly triangulated

annotations.note: Smooth triangulation of manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Integration via partitions of unity

anchor.snippet: use a partition of unity

annotations.note: Smooth partitions of unity exist (`SmoothPartitionOfUnity`), but no integration of forms via partitions of unity is defined.

annotations.label: Fibral part ω_x / η_y of a form

anchor.snippet: which may be thought of as the fibral part

annotations.note: Fibral decomposition of forms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Generalized Fubini formula

anchor.snippet: the generalized Fubini formula

annotations.note: The Fubini formula for fiber integration of forms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Integration along fibers (higher degree)

anchor.snippet: It is also possible to integrate forms of other degrees along the fibers

annotations.note: Fiber integration of higher-degree forms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Projection formula for fiber integration

anchor.snippet: Integration along fibers satisfies the projection formula

annotations.note: The projection formula for fiber integration is not formalized.

annotations.label: Stokes' theorem

anchor.snippet: is given by the Stokes' theorem

annotations.note: Only the divergence theorem on rectangles is formalized; the general Stokes theorem `∫_∂Ω ω = ∫_Ω dω` for differential forms is not.

annotations.label: Integral of a closed form over homologous chains is equal

anchor.snippet: the integral of a closed form over homologous chains is equal

annotations.note: Equality of integrals of a closed form over homologous chains is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gradient theorem

anchor.snippet: This case is called the gradient theorem

annotations.note: The 1D fundamental theorem of calculus is formalized, but the multivariable gradient theorem on line integrals is not.

annotations.label: Identification of n-forms with densities given an orientation

anchor.snippet: one may identify n -forms with densities on a manifold

annotations.note: No identification of top-degree forms with densities/measures is formalized.

annotations.label: Current

anchor.snippet: is called a current

annotations.note: Currents (distributional differential forms) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Faraday 2-form (electromagnetic field strength)

anchor.snippet: the Faraday 2-form

annotations.note: The Faraday 2-form is not formalized.

annotations.label: Current 3-form

anchor.snippet: The current 3 -form is

annotations.note: The electromagnetic current 3-form is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maxwell's equations in form notation

anchor.snippet: Maxwell's equations can be written very compactly

annotations.note: Maxwell's equations in differential-form notation are not formalized.

annotations.label: Yang–Mills field

anchor.snippet: The Yang–Mills field

annotations.note: Yang–Mills fields are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wirtinger inequality for 2-forms

anchor.snippet: the Wirtinger inequality for 2-forms

annotations.note: The Wirtinger inequality for 2-forms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gromov's inequality for complex projective space

anchor.snippet: Gromov's inequality for complex projective space

annotations.note: Gromov's systolic inequality for complex projective space is not formalized.
